prompt:
Develop a Clinical Operations Portal system for a multi-campus healthcare organization to manage internal property listings, clinical knowledge content, and training-class feedback in one offline-capable web experience. Staff users sign in with a locally verified username and password and navigate a responsive, English-language interface where HTMX progressively updates pages (inline saves, modal previews, and status badges) without full reloads, even on older clinic workstations. Property Managers create and maintain the full lifecycle of housing and office listings used by rotating clinicians, capturing address, floor plan notes, square footage, amenities, monthly rent and deposit in USD, and lease-term availability windows, then submit for review before publishing; listings support publish/unpublish/lock actions and a clear status workflow so expired or non-compliant listings cannot be booked or edited. Instructors ("coaches") run in-person training classes (e.g., medication safety refreshers) and receive class reviews from verified attendees using a 1-5 star rating, up to five predefined tags, and optional text (20-1,000 characters); coaches can post one threaded reply per review, while configurable settings let Org Admins choose whether reviewer names display as full name, initials, or anonymous. Content Moderators handle non-compliant review reports using basic, offline keyword rules and manual review, with an appeal flow that must be filed within 14 calendar days and resolved within 5 business days, and the UI shows clear feedback when a review is hidden, restored, or finalized.

The backend uses Flask to enforce business rules, validate inputs, and expose decoupled REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX, with SQLite providing durable local persistence for users, organizations, listings, reviews, drugs, tags, and audit trails. The permissions model supports a multi-level hierarchy (campus-college-department-section) and RBAC down to feature access and data scope, including restrictions by organization unit, asset category, and listing status; temporary grants must include an explicit reason and auto-expire after a configurable duration (default 72 hours), and permission audit reports are exportable for compliance review. Drug and knowledge base management supports bulk CSV import with duplicate detection (generic name + strength + form), controlled vocabularies for tag taxonomy, full-text search via SQLite FTS, and a publish-approval workflow where only approved content becomes visible. Security and compliance include immutable audit logs for key actions such as scheduling changes, price updates, refunds, and permission changes; sensitive fields are masked in the UI for non-privileged roles and encrypted at rest (AES-GCM) with separate keys per environment to ensure multi-environment isolation. API requests are signed with HMAC and protected against replay using a timestamp and nonce with a 5-minute acceptance window. High availability is addressed through offline-first behavior: the browser caches read-most screens and queues write intents during poor connectivity, the server retries failed operations with exponential backoff, and a lightweight internal message queue backed by SQLite processes asynchronous jobs. Encrypted backups run nightly with 30-day retention, restores are testable from the admin console, and the disaster recovery plan targets a 24-hour RPO and a 4-hour RTO on local infrastructure.
